query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
-- GED-MATH Lesson 1: Number Operations (570001)
(570001, 'The order of operations is remembered by:', 'multiple_choice', '["BODMAS or PEMDAS","Random order","Left to right only","Right to left only"]', 'BODMAS or PEMDAS', 1),
(570001, 'In PEMDAS, E stands for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Equals","Exponents","Estimation","Elimination"]', 'Exponents', 2),
(570001, 'When adding fractions with different denominators, you must first:', 'multiple_choice', '["Add the denominators","Find a common denominator","Multiply the numerators","Ignore the denominators"]', 'Find a common denominator', 3),
(570001, 'A negative times a negative equals:', 'multiple_choice', '["Negative","Positive","Zero","Undefined"]', 'Positive', 4),
(570001, 'To convert a fraction to a decimal, you:', 'multiple_choice', '["Add numerator and denominator","Divide numerator by denominator","Multiply numerator by denominator","Subtract denominator from numerator"]', 'Divide numerator by denominator', 5),
(570001, 'The absolute value of -7 is:', 'multiple_choice', '["-7","7","0","14"]', '7', 6),
(570001, 'When dividing by a fraction, you should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Divide normally","Multiply by the reciprocal","Add the fractions","Subtract the fractions"]', 'Multiply by the reciprocal', 7),
(570001, 'A prime number is divisible only by:', 'multiple_choice', '["1 and itself","Any number","Even numbers","Odd numbers"]', '1 and itself', 8),
(570001, 'The least common multiple (LCM) of 4 and 6 is:', 'multiple_choice', '["2","12","24","10"]', '12', 9),
(570001, 'Rounding 3.456 to the nearest tenth gives:', 'multiple_choice', '["3.4","3.5","3.46","3.45"]', '3.5', 10),
(570001, 'Explain the order of operations and why it is important in mathematics.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Order of operations (PEMDAS) ensures consistent results by specifying the sequence: Parentheses, Exponents, Multiplication/Division, Addition/Subtraction.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 2: Algebraic Expressions (570002)
(570002, 'A variable is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A fixed number","A letter representing an unknown value","An operation","A constant"]', 'A letter representing an unknown value', 1),
(570002, 'Like terms have:', 'multiple_choice', '["Different variables","The same variables with the same exponents","No variables","Different exponents"]', 'The same variables with the same exponents', 2),
(570002, 'To simplify 3x + 5x, you get:', 'multiple_choice', '["8x","15x","8x²","35x"]', '8x', 3),
(570002, 'The distributive property states that a(b + c) equals:', 'multiple_choice', '["ab + c","a + bc","ab + ac","abc"]', 'ab + ac', 4),
(570002, 'In the expression 5x², the coefficient is:', 'multiple_choice', '["x","2","5","5x"]', '5', 5),
(570002, 'Evaluating an expression means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Simplifying it","Substituting values for variables","Factoring it","Graphing it"]', 'Substituting values for variables', 6),
(570002, 'The expression 2(x + 3) simplified is:', 'multiple_choice', '["2x + 3","2x + 6","x + 6","2x + 5"]', '2x + 6', 7),
(570002, 'A constant is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A changing value","A fixed numerical value","A variable","An operation"]', 'A fixed numerical value', 8),
(570002, 'Combining 4y - 2y gives:', 'multiple_choice', '["6y","2y","2","8y"]', '2y', 9),
(570002, 'An exponent indicates:', 'multiple_choice', '["Addition","Repeated multiplication","Division","Subtraction"]', 'Repeated multiplication', 10),
(570002, 'Explain how to simplify algebraic expressions by combining like terms.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Combine like terms by adding or subtracting coefficients of terms with the same variables and exponents.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 3: Linear Equations (570003)
(570003, 'A linear equation has variables raised to the power of:', 'multiple_choice', '["2","0","1","3"]', '1', 1),
(570003, 'To solve for x in x + 5 = 12, you:', 'multiple_choice', '["Add 5 to both sides","Subtract 5 from both sides","Multiply both sides by 5","Divide both sides by 5"]', 'Subtract 5 from both sides', 2),
(570003, 'The solution to 3x = 15 is:', 'multiple_choice', '["x = 45","x = 5","x = 12","x = 18"]', 'x = 5', 3),
(570003, 'When solving equations, you must:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only work on one side","Perform the same operation on both sides","Ignore the equals sign","Change the variable"]', 'Perform the same operation on both sides', 4),
(570003, 'In 2x + 3 = 11, the first step is to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Divide by 2","Subtract 3 from both sides","Add 3 to both sides","Multiply by 2"]', 'Subtract 3 from both sides', 5),
(570003, 'The equation y = mx + b represents:', 'multiple_choice', '["A circle","A line","A parabola","A point"]', 'A line', 6),
(570003, 'In y = mx + b, m represents:', 'multiple_choice', '["The y-intercept","The slope","The x-intercept","A variable"]', 'The slope', 7),
(570003, 'Solving 4x - 8 = 0 gives x =:', 'multiple_choice', '["2","-2","8","4"]', '2', 8),
(570003, 'An equation with no solution is called:', 'multiple_choice', '["Consistent","Inconsistent","Linear","Quadratic"]', 'Inconsistent', 9),
(570003, 'Checking your solution means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Guessing","Substituting back into the original equation","Ignoring the answer","Solving again"]', 'Substituting back into the original equation', 10),
(570003, 'Describe the steps to solve a linear equation.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Steps include isolating the variable by performing inverse operations on both sides, then checking the solution.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 4: Ratios and Proportions (570004)
(570004, 'A ratio compares:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only equal quantities","Two quantities","One quantity","Three or more quantities"]', 'Two quantities', 1),
(570004, 'A proportion is:', 'multiple_choice', '["An unequal ratio","An equation stating two ratios are equal","A single ratio","A fraction"]', 'An equation stating two ratios are equal', 2),
(570004, 'In the ratio 3:4, the first term is:', 'multiple_choice', '["4","3","7","12"]', '3', 3),
(570004, 'Cross multiplication is used to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Add fractions","Solve proportions","Find averages","Multiply fractions"]', 'Solve proportions', 4),
(570004, 'If 2/3 = x/9, then x equals:', 'multiple_choice', '["3","6","9","12"]', '6', 5),
(570004, 'A unit rate has a denominator of:', 'multiple_choice', '["0","1","10","100"]', '1', 6),
(570004, 'Scale drawings use:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random sizes","Proportional relationships","Equal measurements","No measurements"]', 'Proportional relationships', 7),
(570004, 'If 5 items cost $15, the unit price is:', 'multiple_choice', '["$15","$3","$5","$75"]', '$3', 8),
(570004, 'Percent means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Per thousand","Per hundred","Per ten","Per million"]', 'Per hundred', 9),
(570004, 'To find 20% of 50, you calculate:', 'multiple_choice', '["20 × 50","0.20 × 50","20 + 50","50 ÷ 20"]', '0.20 × 50', 10),
(570004, 'Explain how to solve proportion problems using cross multiplication.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Cross multiply by multiplying the numerator of each fraction by the denominator of the other, then solve for the unknown.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 5: Geometry Basics (570005)
(570005, 'The perimeter of a rectangle is calculated by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Length × Width","2(Length + Width)","Length + Width","Length ÷ Width"]', '2(Length + Width)', 1),
(570005, 'The area of a rectangle is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Length + Width","Length × Width","2(Length + Width)","Length - Width"]', 'Length × Width', 2),
(570005, 'A right angle measures:', 'multiple_choice', '["45 degrees","90 degrees","180 degrees","360 degrees"]', '90 degrees', 3),
(570005, 'The sum of angles in a triangle is:', 'multiple_choice', '["90 degrees","180 degrees","270 degrees","360 degrees"]', '180 degrees', 4),
(570005, 'The Pythagorean theorem states:', 'multiple_choice', '["a + b = c","a² + b² = c²","a × b = c","a - b = c"]', 'a² + b² = c²', 5),
(570005, 'The area of a circle is:', 'multiple_choice', '["πd","2πr","πr²","πr"]', 'πr²', 6),
(570005, 'Circumference of a circle is:', 'multiple_choice', '["πr²","2πr","πd²","r²"]', '2πr', 7),
(570005, 'Volume of a rectangular prism is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Length × Width","Length × Width × Height","2(L + W + H)","L + W + H"]', 'Length × Width × Height', 8),
(570005, 'Parallel lines:', 'multiple_choice', '["Intersect at one point","Never intersect","Are perpendicular","Form right angles"]', 'Never intersect', 9),
(570005, 'A polygon with 6 sides is called a:', 'multiple_choice', '["Pentagon","Hexagon","Heptagon","Octagon"]', 'Hexagon', 10),
(570005, 'Explain the Pythagorean theorem and when to use it.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'The Pythagorean theorem (a² + b² = c²) applies to right triangles to find the length of a side when two sides are known.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 6: Data Analysis (570006)
(570006, 'The mean is calculated by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Finding the middle value","Summing values and dividing by count","Finding the most frequent value","Subtracting smallest from largest"]', 'Summing values and dividing by count', 1),
(570006, 'The median is:', 'multiple_choice', '["The average","The middle value when data is ordered","The most frequent value","The range"]', 'The middle value when data is ordered', 2),
(570006, 'The mode is:', 'multiple_choice', '["The average","The middle value","The most frequently occurring value","The difference"]', 'The most frequently occurring value', 3),
(570006, 'Range is calculated by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Adding all values","Subtracting minimum from maximum","Finding the average","Counting values"]', 'Subtracting minimum from maximum', 4),
(570006, 'A bar graph is best for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Showing trends over time","Comparing categories","Showing parts of a whole","Displaying continuous data"]', 'Comparing categories', 5),
(570006, 'A pie chart shows:', 'multiple_choice', '["Trends over time","Parts of a whole","Comparisons between groups","Scatter plots"]', 'Parts of a whole', 6),
(570006, 'A line graph is best for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Comparing categories","Showing trends over time","Showing parts of a whole","Displaying frequency"]', 'Showing trends over time', 7),
(570006, 'Probability is expressed as:', 'multiple_choice', '["A number greater than 1","A number between 0 and 1","A negative number","An undefined value"]', 'A number between 0 and 1', 8),
(570006, 'If you flip a fair coin, the probability of heads is:', 'multiple_choice', '["0","1","0.5","0.25"]', '0.5', 9),
(570006, 'An outlier is:', 'multiple_choice', '["The average value","A value far from other data points","The most common value","The middle value"]', 'A value far from other data points', 10),
(570006, 'Explain the difference between mean, median, and mode.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Mean is the average, median is the middle value when ordered, and mode is the most frequent value in a dataset.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 7: Functions and Graphs (570007)
(570007, 'A function assigns:', 'multiple_choice', '["Multiple outputs to one input","Exactly one output to each input","No outputs","Random outputs"]', 'Exactly one output to each input', 1),
(570007, 'In f(x), x is called the:', 'multiple_choice', '["Output","Input","Function","Constant"]', 'Input', 2),
(570007, 'The vertical line test determines if a graph is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Linear","A function","Quadratic","Exponential"]', 'A function', 3),
(570007, 'The domain of a function is:', 'multiple_choice', '["All possible outputs","All possible inputs","The range","The slope"]', 'All possible inputs', 4),
(570007, 'The range of a function is:', 'multiple_choice', '["All possible inputs","All possible outputs","The domain","The intercept"]', 'All possible outputs', 5),
(570007, 'A linear function has a graph that is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A curve","A straight line","A parabola","A circle"]', 'A straight line', 6),
(570007, 'The y-intercept is where the graph crosses:', 'multiple_choice', '["The x-axis","The y-axis","The origin","No axis"]', 'The y-axis', 7),
(570007, 'Slope represents:', 'multiple_choice', '["The y-intercept","The rate of change","The x-intercept","A constant"]', 'The rate of change', 8),
(570007, 'A positive slope means the line goes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Down from left to right","Up from left to right","Horizontal","Vertical"]', 'Up from left to right', 9),
(570007, 'If f(x) = 2x + 3, then f(4) equals:', 'multiple_choice', '["8","11","14","7"]', '11', 10),
(570007, 'Explain what a function is and how to determine if a graph represents a function.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'A function assigns exactly one output to each input. Use the vertical line test - if any vertical line crosses the graph more than once, it is not a function.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 8: Quadratic Equations (570008)
(570008, 'A quadratic equation has the form:', 'multiple_choice', '["ax + b = 0","ax² + bx + c = 0","ax³ + bx² + cx + d = 0","a/x = b"]', 'ax² + bx + c = 0', 1),
(570008, 'The graph of a quadratic function is called a:', 'multiple_choice', '["Line","Parabola","Circle","Hyperbola"]', 'Parabola', 2),
(570008, 'The vertex of a parabola is:', 'multiple_choice', '["The y-intercept","The highest or lowest point","The x-intercept","A random point"]', 'The highest or lowest point', 3),
(570008, 'Factoring x² + 5x + 6 gives:', 'multiple_choice', '["(x + 2)(x + 3)","(x + 1)(x + 6)","(x - 2)(x - 3)","(x + 5)(x + 1)"]', '(x + 2)(x + 3)', 4),
(570008, 'The quadratic formula is:', 'multiple_choice', '["x = -b/2a","x = (-b ± √(b² - 4ac))/2a","x = b² - 4ac","x = a + b + c"]', 'x = (-b ± √(b² - 4ac))/2a', 5),
(570008, 'The discriminant (b² - 4ac) determines:', 'multiple_choice', '["The vertex","The number and type of solutions","The y-intercept","The axis of symmetry"]', 'The number and type of solutions', 6),
(570008, 'If the discriminant is positive, there are:', 'multiple_choice', '["No real solutions","One real solution","Two real solutions","Infinite solutions"]', 'Two real solutions', 7),
(570008, 'If the discriminant is zero, there is:', 'multiple_choice', '["No real solution","One real solution","Two real solutions","Infinite solutions"]', 'One real solution', 8),
(570008, 'The axis of symmetry passes through:', 'multiple_choice', '["The y-intercept","The vertex","The x-intercepts","Random points"]', 'The vertex', 9),
(570008, 'To solve x² = 16, x equals:', 'multiple_choice', '["4 only","-4 only","4 or -4","8"]', '4 or -4', 10),
(570008, 'Explain methods for solving quadratic equations.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Methods include factoring, using the quadratic formula, completing the square, and graphing to find x-intercepts.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 9: Word Problems (570009)
(570009, 'The first step in solving word problems is to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Start calculating","Read and understand the problem","Guess the answer","Skip to the end"]', 'Read and understand the problem', 1),
(570009, 'Key words like sum, total, and altogether indicate:', 'multiple_choice', '["Subtraction","Addition","Multiplication","Division"]', 'Addition', 2),
(570009, 'Key words like difference, less than, and remain indicate:', 'multiple_choice', '["Addition","Subtraction","Multiplication","Division"]', 'Subtraction', 3),
(570009, 'Key words like product, times, and of indicate:', 'multiple_choice', '["Addition","Subtraction","Multiplication","Division"]', 'Multiplication', 4),
(570009, 'Key words like quotient, per, and ratio indicate:', 'multiple_choice', '["Addition","Subtraction","Multiplication","Division"]', 'Division', 5),
(570009, 'Defining variables means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Guessing numbers","Assigning letters to unknown quantities","Ignoring unknowns","Using only numbers"]', 'Assigning letters to unknown quantities', 6),
(570009, 'After solving, you should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Stop immediately","Check if the answer makes sense","Ignore the result","Change the answer"]', 'Check if the answer makes sense', 7),
(570009, 'Drawing a diagram can help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Waste time","Visualize the problem","Confuse you","Nothing"]', 'Visualize the problem', 8),
(570009, 'Unit analysis ensures:', 'multiple_choice', '["Wrong answers","Correct units in the final answer","No units","Random units"]', 'Correct units in the final answer', 9),
(570009, 'Breaking complex problems into steps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Makes them harder","Makes them more manageable","Is unnecessary","Should be avoided"]', 'Makes them more manageable', 10),
(570009, 'Describe a systematic approach to solving word problems.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Read carefully, identify knowns and unknowns, define variables, set up equations, solve, and check the answer.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 10: Test Strategies (570010)
(570010, 'Time management on the GED Math test means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Spending all time on hard problems","Pacing yourself through all questions","Rushing through everything","Ignoring time"]', 'Pacing yourself through all questions', 1),
(570010, 'The GED calculator should be used:', 'multiple_choice', '["For every problem","Strategically when helpful","Never","Only for addition"]', 'Strategically when helpful', 2),
(570010, 'Estimation can help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Waste time","Eliminate wrong answers quickly","Nothing","Confuse you"]', 'Eliminate wrong answers quickly', 3),
(570010, 'Reading math questions carefully helps avoid:', 'multiple_choice', '["Understanding","Careless errors","Correct answers","Good scores"]', 'Careless errors', 4),
(570010, 'If stuck on a problem, you should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Give up","Skip it and return later","Spend unlimited time","Leave it blank"]', 'Skip it and return later', 5),
(570010, 'Showing your work helps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Waste time","Track your steps and find errors","Nothing","Confuse you"]', 'Track your steps and find errors', 6),
(570010, 'Plugging answers back in verifies:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Your solution is correct","The problem is wrong","Time wasted"]', 'Your solution is correct', 7),
(570010, 'Common math mistakes include:', 'multiple_choice', '["Being careful","Sign errors and calculation mistakes","Reading carefully","Checking work"]', 'Sign errors and calculation mistakes', 8),
(570010, 'Practice tests help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Increase anxiety","Build familiarity and confidence","Nothing","Waste time"]', 'Build familiarity and confidence', 9),
(570010, 'Staying calm during the test:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is impossible","Helps you think clearly","Wastes time","Is unnecessary"]', 'Helps you think clearly', 10),
(570010, 'Describe effective strategies for the GED Math test.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Strategies include time management, using the calculator wisely, estimation, careful reading, and checking work.', 11);

mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway02.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 2KiCrCqNDFcXt7P.577d1a63eeb5 --database P2Dpw9MQsmxzdUQCfGjV6b --execute INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
-- GED-MATH Lesson 1: Number Operations (570001)
(570001, 'The order of operations is remembered by:', 'multiple_choice', '["BODMAS or PEMDAS","Random order","Left to right only","Right to left only"]', 'BODMAS or PEMDAS', 1),
(570001, 'In PEMDAS, E stands for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Equals","Exponents","Estimation","Elimination"]', 'Exponents', 2),
(570001, 'When adding fractions with different denominators, you must first:', 'multiple_choice', '["Add the denominators","Find a common denominator","Multiply the numerators","Ignore the denominators"]', 'Find a common denominator', 3),
(570001, 'A negative times a negative equals:', 'multiple_choice', '["Negative","Positive","Zero","Undefined"]', 'Positive', 4),
(570001, 'To convert a fraction to a decimal, you:', 'multiple_choice', '["Add numerator and denominator","Divide numerator by denominator","Multiply numerator by denominator","Subtract denominator from numerator"]', 'Divide numerator by denominator', 5),
(570001, 'The absolute value of -7 is:', 'multiple_choice', '["-7","7","0","14"]', '7', 6),
(570001, 'When dividing by a fraction, you should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Divide normally","Multiply by the reciprocal","Add the fractions","Subtract the fractions"]', 'Multiply by the reciprocal', 7),
(570001, 'A prime number is divisible only by:', 'multiple_choice', '["1 and itself","Any number","Even numbers","Odd numbers"]', '1 and itself', 8),
(570001, 'The least common multiple (LCM) of 4 and 6 is:', 'multiple_choice', '["2","12","24","10"]', '12', 9),
(570001, 'Rounding 3.456 to the nearest tenth gives:', 'multiple_choice', '["3.4","3.5","3.46","3.45"]', '3.5', 10),
(570001, 'Explain the order of operations and why it is important in mathematics.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Order of operations (PEMDAS) ensures consistent results by specifying the sequence: Parentheses, Exponents, Multiplication/Division, Addition/Subtraction.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 2: Algebraic Expressions (570002)
(570002, 'A variable is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A fixed number","A letter representing an unknown value","An operation","A constant"]', 'A letter representing an unknown value', 1),
(570002, 'Like terms have:', 'multiple_choice', '["Different variables","The same variables with the same exponents","No variables","Different exponents"]', 'The same variables with the same exponents', 2),
(570002, 'To simplify 3x + 5x, you get:', 'multiple_choice', '["8x","15x","8x²","35x"]', '8x', 3),
(570002, 'The distributive property states that a(b + c) equals:', 'multiple_choice', '["ab + c","a + bc","ab + ac","abc"]', 'ab + ac', 4),
(570002, 'In the expression 5x², the coefficient is:', 'multiple_choice', '["x","2","5","5x"]', '5', 5),
(570002, 'Evaluating an expression means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Simplifying it","Substituting values for variables","Factoring it","Graphing it"]', 'Substituting values for variables', 6),
(570002, 'The expression 2(x + 3) simplified is:', 'multiple_choice', '["2x + 3","2x + 6","x + 6","2x + 5"]', '2x + 6', 7),
(570002, 'A constant is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A changing value","A fixed numerical value","A variable","An operation"]', 'A fixed numerical value', 8),
(570002, 'Combining 4y - 2y gives:', 'multiple_choice', '["6y","2y","2","8y"]', '2y', 9),
(570002, 'An exponent indicates:', 'multiple_choice', '["Addition","Repeated multiplication","Division","Subtraction"]', 'Repeated multiplication', 10),
(570002, 'Explain how to simplify algebraic expressions by combining like terms.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Combine like terms by adding or subtracting coefficients of terms with the same variables and exponents.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 3: Linear Equations (570003)
(570003, 'A linear equation has variables raised to the power of:', 'multiple_choice', '["2","0","1","3"]', '1', 1),
(570003, 'To solve for x in x + 5 = 12, you:', 'multiple_choice', '["Add 5 to both sides","Subtract 5 from both sides","Multiply both sides by 5","Divide both sides by 5"]', 'Subtract 5 from both sides', 2),
(570003, 'The solution to 3x = 15 is:', 'multiple_choice', '["x = 45","x = 5","x = 12","x = 18"]', 'x = 5', 3),
(570003, 'When solving equations, you must:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only work on one side","Perform the same operation on both sides","Ignore the equals sign","Change the variable"]', 'Perform the same operation on both sides', 4),
(570003, 'In 2x + 3 = 11, the first step is to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Divide by 2","Subtract 3 from both sides","Add 3 to both sides","Multiply by 2"]', 'Subtract 3 from both sides', 5),
(570003, 'The equation y = mx + b represents:', 'multiple_choice', '["A circle","A line","A parabola","A point"]', 'A line', 6),
(570003, 'In y = mx + b, m represents:', 'multiple_choice', '["The y-intercept","The slope","The x-intercept","A variable"]', 'The slope', 7),
(570003, 'Solving 4x - 8 = 0 gives x =:', 'multiple_choice', '["2","-2","8","4"]', '2', 8),
(570003, 'An equation with no solution is called:', 'multiple_choice', '["Consistent","Inconsistent","Linear","Quadratic"]', 'Inconsistent', 9),
(570003, 'Checking your solution means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Guessing","Substituting back into the original equation","Ignoring the answer","Solving again"]', 'Substituting back into the original equation', 10),
(570003, 'Describe the steps to solve a linear equation.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Steps include isolating the variable by performing inverse operations on both sides, then checking the solution.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 4: Ratios and Proportions (570004)
(570004, 'A ratio compares:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only equal quantities","Two quantities","One quantity","Three or more quantities"]', 'Two quantities', 1),
(570004, 'A proportion is:', 'multiple_choice', '["An unequal ratio","An equation stating two ratios are equal","A single ratio","A fraction"]', 'An equation stating two ratios are equal', 2),
(570004, 'In the ratio 3:4, the first term is:', 'multiple_choice', '["4","3","7","12"]', '3', 3),
(570004, 'Cross multiplication is used to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Add fractions","Solve proportions","Find averages","Multiply fractions"]', 'Solve proportions', 4),
(570004, 'If 2/3 = x/9, then x equals:', 'multiple_choice', '["3","6","9","12"]', '6', 5),
(570004, 'A unit rate has a denominator of:', 'multiple_choice', '["0","1","10","100"]', '1', 6),
(570004, 'Scale drawings use:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random sizes","Proportional relationships","Equal measurements","No measurements"]', 'Proportional relationships', 7),
(570004, 'If 5 items cost $15, the unit price is:', 'multiple_choice', '["$15","$3","$5","$75"]', '$3', 8),
(570004, 'Percent means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Per thousand","Per hundred","Per ten","Per million"]', 'Per hundred', 9),
(570004, 'To find 20% of 50, you calculate:', 'multiple_choice', '["20 × 50","0.20 × 50","20 + 50","50 ÷ 20"]', '0.20 × 50', 10),
(570004, 'Explain how to solve proportion problems using cross multiplication.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Cross multiply by multiplying the numerator of each fraction by the denominator of the other, then solve for the unknown.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 5: Geometry Basics (570005)
(570005, 'The perimeter of a rectangle is calculated by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Length × Width","2(Length + Width)","Length + Width","Length ÷ Width"]', '2(Length + Width)', 1),
(570005, 'The area of a rectangle is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Length + Width","Length × Width","2(Length + Width)","Length - Width"]', 'Length × Width', 2),
(570005, 'A right angle measures:', 'multiple_choice', '["45 degrees","90 degrees","180 degrees","360 degrees"]', '90 degrees', 3),
(570005, 'The sum of angles in a triangle is:', 'multiple_choice', '["90 degrees","180 degrees","270 degrees","360 degrees"]', '180 degrees', 4),
(570005, 'The Pythagorean theorem states:', 'multiple_choice', '["a + b = c","a² + b² = c²","a × b = c","a - b = c"]', 'a² + b² = c²', 5),
(570005, 'The area of a circle is:', 'multiple_choice', '["πd","2πr","πr²","πr"]', 'πr²', 6),
(570005, 'Circumference of a circle is:', 'multiple_choice', '["πr²","2πr","πd²","r²"]', '2πr', 7),
(570005, 'Volume of a rectangular prism is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Length × Width","Length × Width × Height","2(L + W + H)","L + W + H"]', 'Length × Width × Height', 8),
(570005, 'Parallel lines:', 'multiple_choice', '["Intersect at one point","Never intersect","Are perpendicular","Form right angles"]', 'Never intersect', 9),
(570005, 'A polygon with 6 sides is called a:', 'multiple_choice', '["Pentagon","Hexagon","Heptagon","Octagon"]', 'Hexagon', 10),
(570005, 'Explain the Pythagorean theorem and when to use it.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'The Pythagorean theorem (a² + b² = c²) applies to right triangles to find the length of a side when two sides are known.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 6: Data Analysis (570006)
(570006, 'The mean is calculated by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Finding the middle value","Summing values and dividing by count","Finding the most frequent value","Subtracting smallest from largest"]', 'Summing values and dividing by count', 1),
(570006, 'The median is:', 'multiple_choice', '["The average","The middle value when data is ordered","The most frequent value","The range"]', 'The middle value when data is ordered', 2),
(570006, 'The mode is:', 'multiple_choice', '["The average","The middle value","The most frequently occurring value","The difference"]', 'The most frequently occurring value', 3),
(570006, 'Range is calculated by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Adding all values","Subtracting minimum from maximum","Finding the average","Counting values"]', 'Subtracting minimum from maximum', 4),
(570006, 'A bar graph is best for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Showing trends over time","Comparing categories","Showing parts of a whole","Displaying continuous data"]', 'Comparing categories', 5),
(570006, 'A pie chart shows:', 'multiple_choice', '["Trends over time","Parts of a whole","Comparisons between groups","Scatter plots"]', 'Parts of a whole', 6),
(570006, 'A line graph is best for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Comparing categories","Showing trends over time","Showing parts of a whole","Displaying frequency"]', 'Showing trends over time', 7),
(570006, 'Probability is expressed as:', 'multiple_choice', '["A number greater than 1","A number between 0 and 1","A negative number","An undefined value"]', 'A number between 0 and 1', 8),
(570006, 'If you flip a fair coin, the probability of heads is:', 'multiple_choice', '["0","1","0.5","0.25"]', '0.5', 9),
(570006, 'An outlier is:', 'multiple_choice', '["The average value","A value far from other data points","The most common value","The middle value"]', 'A value far from other data points', 10),
(570006, 'Explain the difference between mean, median, and mode.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Mean is the average, median is the middle value when ordered, and mode is the most frequent value in a dataset.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 7: Functions and Graphs (570007)
(570007, 'A function assigns:', 'multiple_choice', '["Multiple outputs to one input","Exactly one output to each input","No outputs","Random outputs"]', 'Exactly one output to each input', 1),
(570007, 'In f(x), x is called the:', 'multiple_choice', '["Output","Input","Function","Constant"]', 'Input', 2),
(570007, 'The vertical line test determines if a graph is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Linear","A function","Quadratic","Exponential"]', 'A function', 3),
(570007, 'The domain of a function is:', 'multiple_choice', '["All possible outputs","All possible inputs","The range","The slope"]', 'All possible inputs', 4),
(570007, 'The range of a function is:', 'multiple_choice', '["All possible inputs","All possible outputs","The domain","The intercept"]', 'All possible outputs', 5),
(570007, 'A linear function has a graph that is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A curve","A straight line","A parabola","A circle"]', 'A straight line', 6),
(570007, 'The y-intercept is where the graph crosses:', 'multiple_choice', '["The x-axis","The y-axis","The origin","No axis"]', 'The y-axis', 7),
(570007, 'Slope represents:', 'multiple_choice', '["The y-intercept","The rate of change","The x-intercept","A constant"]', 'The rate of change', 8),
(570007, 'A positive slope means the line goes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Down from left to right","Up from left to right","Horizontal","Vertical"]', 'Up from left to right', 9),
(570007, 'If f(x) = 2x + 3, then f(4) equals:', 'multiple_choice', '["8","11","14","7"]', '11', 10),
(570007, 'Explain what a function is and how to determine if a graph represents a function.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'A function assigns exactly one output to each input. Use the vertical line test - if any vertical line crosses the graph more than once, it is not a function.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 8: Quadratic Equations (570008)
(570008, 'A quadratic equation has the form:', 'multiple_choice', '["ax + b = 0","ax² + bx + c = 0","ax³ + bx² + cx + d = 0","a/x = b"]', 'ax² + bx + c = 0', 1),
(570008, 'The graph of a quadratic function is called a:', 'multiple_choice', '["Line","Parabola","Circle","Hyperbola"]', 'Parabola', 2),
(570008, 'The vertex of a parabola is:', 'multiple_choice', '["The y-intercept","The highest or lowest point","The x-intercept","A random point"]', 'The highest or lowest point', 3),
(570008, 'Factoring x² + 5x + 6 gives:', 'multiple_choice', '["(x + 2)(x + 3)","(x + 1)(x + 6)","(x - 2)(x - 3)","(x + 5)(x + 1)"]', '(x + 2)(x + 3)', 4),
(570008, 'The quadratic formula is:', 'multiple_choice', '["x = -b/2a","x = (-b ± √(b² - 4ac))/2a","x = b² - 4ac","x = a + b + c"]', 'x = (-b ± √(b² - 4ac))/2a', 5),
(570008, 'The discriminant (b² - 4ac) determines:', 'multiple_choice', '["The vertex","The number and type of solutions","The y-intercept","The axis of symmetry"]', 'The number and type of solutions', 6),
(570008, 'If the discriminant is positive, there are:', 'multiple_choice', '["No real solutions","One real solution","Two real solutions","Infinite solutions"]', 'Two real solutions', 7),
(570008, 'If the discriminant is zero, there is:', 'multiple_choice', '["No real solution","One real solution","Two real solutions","Infinite solutions"]', 'One real solution', 8),
(570008, 'The axis of symmetry passes through:', 'multiple_choice', '["The y-intercept","The vertex","The x-intercepts","Random points"]', 'The vertex', 9),
(570008, 'To solve x² = 16, x equals:', 'multiple_choice', '["4 only","-4 only","4 or -4","8"]', '4 or -4', 10),
(570008, 'Explain methods for solving quadratic equations.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Methods include factoring, using the quadratic formula, completing the square, and graphing to find x-intercepts.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 9: Word Problems (570009)
(570009, 'The first step in solving word problems is to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Start calculating","Read and understand the problem","Guess the answer","Skip to the end"]', 'Read and understand the problem', 1),
(570009, 'Key words like sum, total, and altogether indicate:', 'multiple_choice', '["Subtraction","Addition","Multiplication","Division"]', 'Addition', 2),
(570009, 'Key words like difference, less than, and remain indicate:', 'multiple_choice', '["Addition","Subtraction","Multiplication","Division"]', 'Subtraction', 3),
(570009, 'Key words like product, times, and of indicate:', 'multiple_choice', '["Addition","Subtraction","Multiplication","Division"]', 'Multiplication', 4),
(570009, 'Key words like quotient, per, and ratio indicate:', 'multiple_choice', '["Addition","Subtraction","Multiplication","Division"]', 'Division', 5),
(570009, 'Defining variables means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Guessing numbers","Assigning letters to unknown quantities","Ignoring unknowns","Using only numbers"]', 'Assigning letters to unknown quantities', 6),
(570009, 'After solving, you should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Stop immediately","Check if the answer makes sense","Ignore the result","Change the answer"]', 'Check if the answer makes sense', 7),
(570009, 'Drawing a diagram can help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Waste time","Visualize the problem","Confuse you","Nothing"]', 'Visualize the problem', 8),
(570009, 'Unit analysis ensures:', 'multiple_choice', '["Wrong answers","Correct units in the final answer","No units","Random units"]', 'Correct units in the final answer', 9),
(570009, 'Breaking complex problems into steps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Makes them harder","Makes them more manageable","Is unnecessary","Should be avoided"]', 'Makes them more manageable', 10),
(570009, 'Describe a systematic approach to solving word problems.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Read carefully, identify knowns and unknowns, define variables, set up equations, solve, and check the answer.', 11),

-- GED-MATH Lesson 10: Test Strategies (570010)
(570010, 'Time management on the GED Math test means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Spending all time on hard problems","Pacing yourself through all questions","Rushing through everything","Ignoring time"]', 'Pacing yourself through all questions', 1),
(570010, 'The GED calculator should be used:', 'multiple_choice', '["For every problem","Strategically when helpful","Never","Only for addition"]', 'Strategically when helpful', 2),
(570010, 'Estimation can help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Waste time","Eliminate wrong answers quickly","Nothing","Confuse you"]', 'Eliminate wrong answers quickly', 3),
(570010, 'Reading math questions carefully helps avoid:', 'multiple_choice', '["Understanding","Careless errors","Correct answers","Good scores"]', 'Careless errors', 4),
(570010, 'If stuck on a problem, you should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Give up","Skip it and return later","Spend unlimited time","Leave it blank"]', 'Skip it and return later', 5),
(570010, 'Showing your work helps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Waste time","Track your steps and find errors","Nothing","Confuse you"]', 'Track your steps and find errors', 6),
(570010, 'Plugging answers back in verifies:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Your solution is correct","The problem is wrong","Time wasted"]', 'Your solution is correct', 7),
(570010, 'Common math mistakes include:', 'multiple_choice', '["Being careful","Sign errors and calculation mistakes","Reading carefully","Checking work"]', 'Sign errors and calculation mistakes', 8),
(570010, 'Practice tests help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Increase anxiety","Build familiarity and confidence","Nothing","Waste time"]', 'Build familiarity and confidence', 9),
(570010, 'Staying calm during the test:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is impossible","Helps you think clearly","Wastes time","Is unnecessary"]', 'Helps you think clearly', 10),
(570010, 'Describe effective strategies for the GED Math test.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Strategies include time management, using the calculator wisely, estimation, careful reading, and checking work.', 11);
